channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In symbolic vote, Congress directs Trump to remove forces from Iran war
item.description: The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.
item.content:encoded:
The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off
item.description: Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."
item.content:encoded:
Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."

(Image credit: Chris Hondros/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Portugal's Ronaldo shakes off World Cup doubters, scores 2 after sluggish start
item.description: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.
item.content:encoded:
At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.

(Image credit: Michael Steele)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: A Revolutionary War soldier's DNA links him to living relatives
item.description: Continental Army soldier John Pumphrey enlisted as a teenager in 1777 and fought at significant battles before his death in action against the British in Camden, S.C.
item.content:encoded:
Continental Army soldier John Pumphrey enlisted as a teenager in 1777 and fought at significant battles before his death in action against the British in Camden, S.C.

(Image credit: Jeffrey Collins)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Afghan Taliban hold first, closed-door talks with EU on deportations
item.description: Rights groups criticized the meeting, saying it undermines the EU's human rights obligations.
item.content:encoded:
Rights groups criticized the meeting, saying it undermines the EU's human rights obligations.

(Image credit: Thanassis Stavrakis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
item.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Who would pay for Trump's proposed $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund?
item.description: It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."
item.content:encoded:
It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: When falling housing prices are good news — and when they're not
item.description: Denver renters are celebrating falling housing costs. But sometimes cheaper housing is a sign of economic decline. How can you tell the difference?
item.content:encoded:
Denver renters are celebrating falling housing costs. But sometimes cheaper housing is a sign of economic decline. How can you tell the difference?

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: A U.S.-Iran dispute over nuclear inspections clouds work to finalize a war-ending deal
item.description: As U.S.-Iran talks continued, a break in the shipping bottleneck through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be in the works.
item.content:encoded:
As U.S.-Iran talks continued, a break in the shipping bottleneck through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be in the works.

(Image credit: Anjum Naveed)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: People fired over Charlie Kirk posts get big payouts for First Amendment retaliation
item.description: While workers, who were employees in government or public institutions, feel vindicated by how their lawsuits concluded, they are still grappling with the aftermath.
item.content:encoded:
While workers, who were employees in government or public institutions, feel vindicated by how their lawsuits concluded, they are still grappling with the aftermath.

(Image credit: Kathryn Gamble for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan
